projectHighlights.title: Helm
projectHighlights.summary: Multi-agent coordination for AI coding workflows—less wasted compute and fewer merge conflicts when several agents run at once. FastAPI backend, React/TypeScript front end, Amazon Bedrock (Claude) for merge arbitration, fleet deduplication, and guardrails.
projectHighlights.matchup: Concurrent coding agents without duplicate work or messy merges
projectHighlights.surface: Python · FastAPI · React · TypeScript · Amazon Bedrock · AWS

projectHighlights.title: ResuMAX
projectHighlights.summary: AI career assistant with Cognito auth, API Gateway + Lambda backend, DynamoDB and S3, and a Gemini-tuned model for chat. React/Tailwind front end; built to handle real sessions, not just a demo.
projectHighlights.matchup: Production AI with auth, storage, and inference cost in mind
projectHighlights.surface: Python · Gemini · React · Tailwind · Node · AWS
projectHighlights.game: Architecture, model wiring, and UI people trust

projectHighlights.title: Skribbl.IO (Java Sockets)
projectHighlights.summary: Multiplayer drawing game over Java sockets with encrypted messages and multi-client sync. Team project for a networking class—lots of threading and protocol edge cases.
projectHighlights.matchup: Keeping draws in sync when everyone sends at once

projectHighlights.title: npweb — this portfolio
projectHighlights.summary: This site—React and Vite, tennis motif, scroll-snap project reel, and a timeline for overlapping roles. Content lives in JSON so updates stay quick.
projectHighlights.matchup: A portfolio I can update without rewriting components
projectHighlights.game: Layout, motion, and a simple content layer

education.schoolSubtitle: Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering
education.detail: Liberty High School (Renton, WA): Summa Cum Laude, 3.98 GPA · Aug 2021 – June 2025.

items.summary: Built Helm with a team over the weekend: a multi-agent coordination engine for AI coding workflows, using Amazon Bedrock (Claude Haiku/Sonnet) for merge arbitration, fleet deduplication, and guardrails across concurrent agent sessions.

items.org: Ma Lab / Seattle Children’s Research Institute
items.summary: Computational models and simulations of biological systems for system-level behavior; optimization and numerical methods to analyze and parameterize biological processes; translating wet-lab problems into computational frameworks with the team.

EEG acquisition and signal processing for SSVEP-based neural frequency detection in real time; custom EEG headsets and visual stimulus rigs; experimental protocols and tooling for SNR, latency, and decoding reliability.
Deep learning across CNNs, RNNs, and RL agents in PyTorch; end-to-end ML pipelines with Hugging Face + PyTorch on real datasets.
Led weekly algorithm and debugging workshops for 15–30 students—Java fundamentals, DSA, and problem-solving culture.

hero.lede: I’m at the Allen School in Ma Lab at Seattle Children’s (simulations and biological modeling). I wrapped a UW BCI project in May 2026; I also ship side projects—recently Helm (2nd at AWS Hackathon 2026) and ResuMAX in production on AWS.
hero.designNotes: I do best on small teams that ship and explain tradeoffs plainly. Reach out if you’re working on health tech, ML systems, or developer tools—I’m looking for a summer internship and like talking through real problems.

bioSection.title: Off the resume
paragraphs: I grew up playing competitive tennis in Renton. The part that stuck for school and work is moving on after a bad point instead of carrying it into the next one.
These days I split time between Ma Lab work and user-facing builds (React, AWS). Still early in my career, but I like going deep on one problem at a time.
